newsSince: No material AI-relevant company news in the June 9-23 window. A widely-cited Blue Point low-carbon ammonia gas-supply contract (>$400M, Louisiana, 2029 startup) is dated June 23, 2025 and is industrial/clean-energy, not semiconductor or data-center. Q1 2026 (May 1) sales +8% predates the window. The electronics-gas-for-fabs thesis is unchanged.

sources.label: Linde Blue Point $400M ammonia gas contract (Yahoo)

verdicts.rankRationale: Upstream pick-and-shovel via electronics/semiconductor gases. No new AI-relevant evidence since June 9; the headline ammonia contract is non-AI and dated 2025. A near-flat day (+0.45%) and +20.7% YTD with near-unanimous Buys is continued rerating, not fresh fundamentals. Hold at rank 42.
verdicts.bull: Industrial-gas oligopoly with rising data-center cooling (LN2/CO2) and fab electronics-gas demand; long-term contracts.
verdicts.neutral: Mega-cap defensive compounder; AI exposure real but small share of mix.
verdicts.bear: $239B cap, +20.7% YTD, fully covered; limited scarce-bottleneck alpha.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Electronics-gas supply wins at leading-edge fabs; liquid-cooling gas demand.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Cyclical industrial volumes; valuation already reflects steady growth.
